title: I Strove With None
content:
I strove with none, for none was worth my strife.
Nature I loved and, next to Nature, Art:
I warm'd both hands before the fire of life;
It sinks, and I am ready to depart.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Walter Savage Landor delivers a powerful performance in "I Strove With None"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
